title: Fix 2D map schedule item markers in Travelogue

requirements.summary: Fix 2D map rendering on first click without requiring hard refresh
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the 2D map button in the Travelogue panel displays the map on the first click without any hard refresh
The map tiles and markers are fully visible immediately after the panel opens
No change to layout, UI structure, or other components

file_operations.description: Add a useEffect hook that calls map.invalidateSize() when the map instance becomes available and after the container is mounted and visible. This ensures Leaflet recalculates the map dimensions after the Travelogue panel opens and the map container is rendered. The effect should depend on the map ref to trigger when the map is first initialized.

requirements.summary: Fix 2D map to show markers from all journeys sharing the same city
acceptanceCriteria: When two or more journeys share the same city, markers from all journeys for that city appear on the 2D map
Markers are uniquely keyed per schedule item (e.g., by schedule item ID or journey+item combination), not by city name alone
No markers from any journey are silently overwritten or skipped
No change to layout, UI structure, or other components

file_operations.description: Change the marker rendering logic for schedule items to use a unique key per schedule item instead of deduplicating by city name. Use a combination of date, time, and location to create unique keys (e.g., `${item.date}-${item.time}-${item.location}`) so that multiple journeys with the same city produce distinct markers. Update the map marker loop to ensure all schedule items are rendered without any city-based deduplication.
